records.ety: [F. apte, L. aptus, fr. obsolete apere to fasten, to join, to fit, akin to apisci to reach, attain: cf. Gr. <?/ to fasten, Skr. āpta fit, fr. āp to reach attain.]

defs: Fit or fitted; suited; suitable; appropriate.
quotes: A river . . . apt to be forded by a lamb.

defs: Having an habitual tendency; habitually liable or likely; -- used of things.
quotes: My vines and peaches . . . were apt to have a soot or smuttiness upon their leaves and fruit.
This tree, if unprotected, is apt to be stripped of the leaves by a leaf-cutting ant.

defs: Inclined; disposed customarily; given; ready; -- used of persons.
quotes: Apter to give than thou wit be to ask.
That lofty pity with which prosperous folk are apt to remember their grandfathers.

defs: Ready; especially fitted or qualified (to do something); quick to learn; prompt; expert; as, a pupil apt to learn; an apt scholar.
quotes: Live a thousand years, I shall not find myself so apt to die.
I find thee apt . . . Now, Hamlet, hear.

defs: To fit; to suit; to adapt.
quotes: That our speech be apted to edification.
